chunks.title: BrainChip Akida — Neuromorphic Segmentation
chunks.text: BrainChip Akida — Neuromorphic Segmentation. High resolution semantic segmentation mapped onto BrainChip Akida 2.0 neuromorphic silicon via hardware aware model surgery and INT8 quantization aware training.. Metrics: 82% · of SOTA accuracy at 7.5% of the params (4.6M vs 61.3M) · 0.55 mIoU · Cityscapes @ 384² · 0.97 IoU · Portrait128. Tech: Akida 2.0, INT8 QAT, Model Surgery, Halo Tiling, PyTorch, Semantic Segmentation. Built an end to end PyTorch→Akida

chunks.title: BrainChip Akida — Neuromorphic Segmentation
chunks.text: INT8 QAT, Model Surgery, Halo Tiling, PyTorch, Semantic Segmentation. Built an end to end PyTorch→Akida pipeline for high resolution semantic segmentation on the BrainChip Akida 2.0 neuromorphic processor (8 bit integer inference) — bridging standard high res CV workflows with the chip's strict memory, operation, and bit width constraints. Level 2 — Architecture Designed an Akida native UNet (custom decoder: nearest neighbor upsample + concat + double conv) via dedicated

chunks.title: BrainChip Akida — Neuromorphic Segmentation
chunks.text: Akida native UNet (custom decoder: nearest neighbor upsample + concat + double conv) via dedicated model surgery scripts ( ) that strip and replace hardware incompatible ops — standard batch norm and transposed convolutions — so the network maps cleanly onto the Akida fabric. Used MobileNetV2 / MobileNetV4 small backbones through + . A lightweight Akida native preset reached 82% of SOTA accuracy at 7.5% of the parameters . Level

chunks.title: BrainChip Akida — Neuromorphic Segmentation
chunks.text: Akida native preset reached 82% of SOTA accuracy at 7.5% of the parameters . Level 3 — Evidence & method Trained on Cityscapes (19 class, mIoU ≈ 0.55 @ 384²) and Portrait128 (binary, ≈ 0.97 IoU) — multi GPU (RTX A6000), Albumentations augmentation (incl. simulated rain/fog), AdamW, BCE / DiceFocal loss, , resumable checkpoints, torchmetrics IoU. Integrated Quantization Aware Training with BrainChip's + toolchain to convert float32 → INT8 deployable

chunks.title: BrainChip Akida — Neuromorphic Segmentation
chunks.text: IoU. Integrated Quantization Aware Training with BrainChip's + toolchain to convert float32 → INT8 deployable Akida models while recovering the mIoU lost to 8 bit precision. Solved high res edge inference with a "resize for training, tile for inference" strategy and pioneered "halo tiling" (overlapping patch margins) to eliminate tile boundary artifacts during full resolution reconstruction. Write up: "Model Surgery: Getting High Resolution Segmentation onto Neuromorphic Silicon" (see Writing). The

chunks.title: BrainChip Akida — Neuromorphic Segmentation
chunks.text: reconstruction. Write up: "Model Surgery: Getting High Resolution Segmentation onto Neuromorphic Silicon" (see Writing). The internship codebase is proprietary to BrainChip — no public repo.

chunks.title: Neuromorphic-Vision-RWKV
chunks.text: Neuromorphic Vision RWKV. RWKV 6 linear attention controller for autonomous driving, built for O(1) constant state inference on edge and neuromorphic hardware.. Metrics: 6.88 ms · median inference (NVIDIA RTX GPU) · 13.4 M · parameters / 51 MiB FP32 · 3.6 TB · CARLA training corpus. Tech: RWKV, PyTorch, CARLA, Edge AI, Imitation Learning, Neuromorphic. Investigated whether RWKV linear complexity recurrence — a "third way" between Transformers and RNNs,

chunks.title: Neuromorphic-Vision-RWKV
chunks.text: Neuromorphic. Investigated whether RWKV linear complexity recurrence — a "third way" between Transformers and RNNs, with parallel training and O(1) constant state inference structurally aligned to Leaky Integrate and Fire spiking dynamics — can drive a car under edge compute limits. Level 2 — Architecture End to end imitation learning prototype: 3 camera panoramic RGB (160×960) → patch embedding with Q Shift locality recovery → pure PyTorch RWKV 6 (Finch)

chunks.title: Neuromorphic-Vision-RWKV
chunks.text: (160×960) → patch embedding with Q Shift locality recovery → pure PyTorch RWKV 6 (Finch) temporal encoder → gated navigation command fusion → policy heads for steering and merged acceleration, plus auxiliary heads (target speed, driving mode, future waypoints). Trained on a 3.6 TB CARLA corpus (1,099 episodes / 3.6 M frames / 6 towns / 10 weathers) with 32 frame clips and 8 frame burn in. Level 3 —

chunks.title: Neuromorphic-Vision-RWKV
chunks.text: / 10 weathers) with 32 frame clips and 8 frame burn in. Level 3 — Evidence & honest results 13.43 M params , 51.22 MiB FP32, 6.88 ms median latency , 64.62 MiB peak CUDA memory on an NVIDIA RTX GPU — beats the proposal's stretch goals. Best open loop steering MAE 0.054. Defined a custom neuromorphic sparsity proxy ( Accumulate and Fire efficiency, η AF ) and reported it

chunks.title: Neuromorphic-Vision-RWKV
chunks.text: custom neuromorphic sparsity proxy ( Accumulate and Fire efficiency, η AF ) and reported it honestly as a negative result : the model is not yet sparse. Measuring and disclosing this is the point — a variance budget you bound before shipping. Targeted edge/neuromorphic deployment: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano, BrainChip Akida; planned TensorRT INT8/FP16 + SNN conversion. Professional repo: , GitHub Actions CI, pre commit (black/flake8/mypy), 100+ pytest tests with

chunks.title: Neuromorphic-Vision-RWKV
chunks.text: SNN conversion. Professional repo: , GitHub Actions CI, pre commit (black/flake8/mypy), 100+ pytest tests with a coverage gate, XeLaTeX thesis.

chunks.title: Quantum Policy Gradient for CartPole
chunks.text: Quantum Policy Gradient for CartPole. A parameter matched comparison of a variational quantum circuit against a classical policy on CartPole — is a VQC more expressive per parameter?. Metrics: Statistical parity · at 18% fewer parameters · p = 0.81 · paired t test (Cohen's d = 0.10) · 9 seeds · 95% confidence intervals. Tech: PennyLane, VQC, Parameter Shift, REINFORCE, Reinforcement Learning. Implemented a Quantum Policy Gradient agent —

chunks.title: Quantum Policy Gradient for CartPole
chunks.text: Tech: PennyLane, VQC, Parameter Shift, REINFORCE, Reinforcement Learning. Implemented a Quantum Policy Gradient agent — a 4 qubit variational quantum circuit ( 42 params) with data re uploading — and ran a rigorous parameter matched comparison against a classical "Tiny MLP" ( 51 params) on CartPole v1 , isolating whether quantum circuits offer superior expressivity per parameter . Level 2 — Method Both agents trained identically with REINFORCE (γ=0.99, Adam,

chunks.title: Quantum Policy Gradient for CartPole
chunks.text: per parameter . Level 2 — Method Both agents trained identically with REINFORCE (γ=0.99, Adam, return normalization, gradient clipping). The quantum circuit used interleaved with , measuring ⟨Z₀⟩/⟨Z₁⟩ → a hybrid linear layer → softmax policy, built on PennyLane's wrapped as a PyTorch . Trained with both backpropagation and the hardware compatible parameter shift rule to validate physical realizability on NISQ devices. Level 3 — Result The quantum agent reached

chunks.title: Quantum Policy Gradient for CartPole
chunks.text: to validate physical realizability on NISQ devices. Level 3 — Result The quantum agent reached statistical parity with the classical baseline (275±59 vs 267±87 episodes to solve; paired t test p=0.81 , Cohen's d=0.10) using 18% fewer parameters . Barren plateau monitoring (gradient norm tracking) confirmed trainability of the shallow 3 layer / 4 qubit circuit. Protocol: 9 random seeds, 95% CIs, paired t tests + effect size, a 139

chunks.title: Quantum Policy Gradient for CartPole
chunks.text: circuit. Protocol: 9 random seeds, 95% CIs, paired t tests + effect size, a 139 test pytest suite, Conda reproducible environment, GIF demos. Graduate IEEE style LaTeX report.

chunks.title: RRI Orchestrator
chunks.text: RRI Orchestrator. Production multi agent LLM platform that decouples the LLM "brain" from ROS2 ready robot "bodies", built for an RIT social robotics research group.. Metrics: Live · deployed at rri.zylot.tech (access controlled) · GPT 4o · Gemini · Claude · multi provider LLM routing via LiteLLM · RBAC + CI · async FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Cloudflare Zero Trust. Tech: FastAPI, LiteLLM, PostgreSQL, Cloudflare Zero Trust, Multi Agent, ROS2 ready. A

chunks.title: RRI Orchestrator
chunks.text: Cloudflare Zero Trust. Tech: FastAPI, LiteLLM, PostgreSQL, Cloudflare Zero Trust, Multi Agent, ROS2 ready. A production deployed, full stack research platform for studying autonomous LLM driven robot robot interaction. It decouples the LLM "brain" from the robot "body" ( ROS2 ready ), self hosted on a personal Ubuntu server and exposed securely to the internet via Cloudflare Tunnel. Level 2 — Architecture Async FastAPI/Starlette core with LiteLLM multi provider routing

chunks.title: RRI Orchestrator
chunks.text: via Cloudflare Tunnel. Level 2 — Architecture Async FastAPI/Starlette core with LiteLLM multi provider routing (Gemini / OpenAI / Anthropic) featuring dynamic API based model discovery + 24h cache, deprecated model migration, tiktoken token/cost tracking, and context window summarization. PostgreSQL 16 (Tortoise ORM, Aerich migrations). Security via Cloudflare Zero Trust SSO through custom header auth middleware, RBAC (admin/researcher), and an account approval workflow. Background for CSV/JSON batch experiments with concurrency

chunks.title: RRI Orchestrator
chunks.text: middleware, RBAC (admin/researcher), and an account approval workflow. Background for CSV/JSON batch experiments with concurrency control, retry/backoff, and pause/resume. Level 3 — Evidence & honest caveats Engineering: Docker Compose, scheduled backups, a systemd auto start service, GitHub Actions CI/CD, a pytest suite, and dependency management; MVVM ViewModels for zero flicker live batch monitoring. Honest caveat: a cyclic LangGraph + MCP refactor exists as a tested scaffold on a branch (10

chunks.title: RRI Orchestrator
chunks.text: a cyclic LangGraph + MCP refactor exists as a tested scaffold on a branch (10 tests + CI: supervisor↔tool loop, SQLite + PostgreSQL MCP servers, self consistency variance gate, human in the loop interrupt/resume) — it is not yet wired into the live platform . The live deployment is access controlled; the link above reaches the login gated platform.

chunks.title: VGG-16 CUDA Optimization
chunks.text: VGG 16 CUDA Optimization. Custom CUDA C++ kernels for VGG 16 inference on an NVIDIA RTX GPU, with Nsight Compute profiling that pins the FC layers as memory bound.. Metrics: 41.5 GFLOPS · FC layer @ batch=16 ( 16.4× over batch=1) · CGMA 2.0 · vs 156.7 needed → FC memory bound. Tech: CUDA C++, cuBLAS, cuDNN, Nsight Compute, HPC. Optimized VGG 16 convolution and fully connected layers for an

chunks.title: VGG-16 CUDA Optimization
chunks.text: cuBLAS, cuDNN, Nsight Compute, HPC. Optimized VGG 16 convolution and fully connected layers for an NVIDIA RTX GPU (CC 8.6) using custom CUDA kernels alongside cuDNN (conv) and cuBLAS (FC), then profiled with NVIDIA Nsight Compute to locate the true bottleneck. Level 2 — Approach Built tiled and vectorized kernel variants (shared memory tiling, memory coalescing) and benchmarked FC layer throughput across batch sizes — near linear scaling to 41.5

chunks.title: VGG-16 CUDA Optimization
chunks.text: coalescing) and benchmarked FC layer throughput across batch sizes — near linear scaling to 41.5 GFLOPS at batch=16 . A CGMA (compute to global memory access) analysis showed the FC layers sit at CGMA ≈ 2.0 versus the ≈ 156.7 needed to be compute bound, identifying them as memory bound rather than compute bound. A 6 person team split across three sub teams (conv/shared memory tiling, memory coalescing, and kernel

chunks.title: VGG-16 CUDA Optimization
chunks.text: 6 person team split across three sub teams (conv/shared memory tiling, memory coalescing, and kernel fusion/FC + integration). Level 3 — Evidence Roofline / CGMA analysis, per batch GFLOPS curves, and Nsight Compute profiles. Captioned Nsight screenshots to be added as inline evidence.

chunks.title: Shahzeb Jadoon — profile
chunks.text: Shahzeb Jadoon, AI/ML Engineer — Edge AI & Hardware-Software Co-Design. Getting deep models to run efficiently on small, power-constrained hardware. Skills: Edge AI, Hardware-Software Co-Design, Model Quantization (INT8/FP16), Quantization-Aware Training, CUDA / cuBLAS, Neuromorphic Computing, BrainChip Akida, Semantic Segmentation, Autonomous Vehicles, RWKV / Sequence Modeling, PyTorch, Multi-Agent LLM Systems, High Performance Computing. Based in Rochester, NY, USA. Contact shahzeb.jadoon.sj@gmail.com, (585) 202-9088.
